content:
# Zarghat Magnesite Deposit

**Summary**: A magnesite deposit in the Jibalah Group of the Arabian Shield.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[1991-makkawi|Makkawi, 1991]] studied the Zarghat deposit's mineral distribution.

- [[1985-ghaled|Ghaled, 1985]] studied the Zarghat magnesite deposit to characterize its mineralogy and geochemistry.

## Depositional Environment

- [[1991-makkawi|Makkawi, 1991]] sedimentary carbonate deposit

- [[1985-ghaled|Ghaled, 1985]] studied the Zarghat magnesite deposit to characterize its mineralogy and geochemistry.

## Reservoir Properties



## Diagenetic History

- [[1991-makkawi|Makkawi, 1991]] precipitated as hydromagnesite then dehydrated to magnesite

## Key Findings Across Sources

- The Central Hill contains approximately 3 million tons of magnesite with a grade ranging from 38% to 48% MgO. ([[1991-makkawi|Makkawi, 1991, p. 20]])

- The deposit contains approximately 3 million tons of ore with a grade of 38-48% MgO. ([[1985-ghaled|Ghaled, 1985, p. 22]])
